# FHIR Queries: T1D Population for CGM Coverage Initiative

---

## 🩺 Group 1 — T1D Diagnosis Codes (Most Specific Signal)

### 1a. ICD-10-CM: Full E10.\* Hierarchy (`:below` traverses all T1D subtypes)
> Captures: **T1D parent (E10)**, **T1D with foot/skin ulcer (E10.621, E10.622)**, **T1D without retinopathy (E10.9, E10.319)**, and all other E10.x complication codes

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E10
```

### 1b. ICD-10-CM: Pre-Existing T1D in Pregnancy / Puerperium (O24.0x)
> Captures: O24.011–O24.019 (antepartum by trimester), O24.02 (childbirth), O24.03 (puerperium)

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|O24.0
```

### 1c. SNOMED CT: T1D / IDDM Hierarchy (`:below` includes IDDM Type IA immune-mediated subtypes)
> Captures: **46635009** (Type 1 DM, disorder) and all descendant concepts — including Type 1A autoimmune, Type 1B idiopathic, LADA-coded as T1D, juvenile-onset IDDM variants

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009
```

---

## 💉 Group 2 — Insulin Therapy (Broad; Overlaps Heavily with T2D — Cross-Reference Group 1)

> **Covers all major classes:** Basal (glargine, detemir, degludec), Rapid-acting (lispro, aspart, glulisine), Short-acting (regular human), Intermediate (NPH/isophane)

| Insulin | Brand Examples | RxNorm Ingredient RxCUI |
|---|---|---|
| Insulin glargine | Lantus, Basaglar, Toujeo | 274783 |
| Insulin detemir | Levemir | 351297 |
| Insulin degludec | Tresiba | 1551291 |
| Insulin aspart | NovoLog, Fiasp | 325072 |
| Insulin lispro | Humalog, Admelog | 86009 |
| Insulin glulisine | Apidra | 400008 |
| Insulin regular human | Humulin R, Novolin R | 51428 |
| Insulin isophane (NPH) | Humulin N, Novolin N | 51143 |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|274783,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|351297,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1551291,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|325072,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|86009,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|400008,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|51428,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|51143
```

---

## 🧪 Group 3 — Hemoglobin A1c ≥ 6.5% (Broad; Overlaps T2D, Prediabetes)

> Covers the four most prevalent HbA1c LOINC codes across lab platforms (HPLC, immunoassay, calculation, and the general panel code)

| LOINC | Description |
|---|---|
| 4548-4 | Hgb A1c / Hgb.total in Blood (most common, general) |
| 17856-6 | Hgb A1c by HPLC |
| 59261-8 | Hgb A1c by Immunoassay |
| 4549-2 | Hgb A1c in Blood (legacy/older code) |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|59261-8,http://loinc.org|4549-2&value-quantity=ge6.5||%25
```

---

## 📋 Population Assembly Notes

| Query Group | Specificity | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **Group 1 (E10.\*)** | ✅ **Highest** — T1D-specific | Use as **primary inclusion list**; these patients are definitively T1D |
| **Group 1 (O24.0x)** | ✅ **High** — T1D-specific, pregnancy context | Add to primary list; these patients have confirmed pre-existing T1D |
| **Group 1 (SNOMED)** | ✅ **High** — catches IDDM Type IA & non-ICD-coded T1D | Merge with ICD-10 list; deduplicate by patient |
| **Group 2 (Insulin)** | ⚠️ **Low** — substantial T2D overlap | Use to **expand** Group 1 only after filtering against T2D diagnosis codes (E11.\*); identify "insulin-on-T1D-Dx" patients |
| **Group 3 (HbA1c)** | ⚠️ **Very Low** — non-specific | Use to **flag** known T1D patients who are poorly controlled; not reliable for new T1D identification |

> **Recommended union logic:**
> `T1D Population = Group1_patients ∪ (Group2_patients ∩ Group1_patients) ∪ (Group3_patients ∩ Group1_patients)`
>
> Equivalently: anchor on Group 1 diagnoses as the definitive T1D roster; use Groups 2 and 3 to confirm clinical burden and completeness, not to add new T1D patients independently.

> **⚠️ Note on `:below` modifier:** Hierarchical traversal for ICD-10-CM requires that your FHIR server has loaded the ICD-10-CM code system with parent-child relationships. If unsupported, replace with explicit comma-separated individual codes (e.g., `|E10,|E10.9,|E10.621,|E10.622,...`). SNOMED CT `:below` is more broadly supported.

I need to identify my complete type 1 diabetes patient population for a continuous glucose monitor coverage initiative. From clinical experience, T1D patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal T1D diagnosis code, all are on insulin therapy (lifelong, from disease onset), and many have intermittently elevated HbA1c. Note that T2D patients may also be on insulin late in disease — distinguishing T1D from insulin-treated T2D requires the specific T1D diagnosis codes.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any T1D diagnosis (T1D parent, IDDM type IA, T1D with ulcer, pre-existing T1D, or T1D without retinopathy)
2. Patients prescribed insulin formulations
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5 percent

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found. Note that the insulin and HbA1c queries will substantially overlap with T2D — the T1D diagnosis query is the most specific.

1-expert.raw_response:
**Query 1B — ICD-10-CM O24.0 Family** *(covers: pre-existing T1D in pregnancy · in childbirth · in puerperium — O24.011–O24.019, O24.02, O24.03)*
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|O24.0&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Query 1C — SNOMED CT Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus** *(covers: all T1D subtypes via `:below` — autoimmune T1D, T1D with complications, brittle diabetes, etc.)*
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### 🟡 GROUP 2 — Insulin Prescriptions *(Broad — Overlaps with Insulin-Treated T2D; Cross-Reference Against Group 1)*

**Query 2A — Rapid-Acting Insulin Analogs** *(insulin lispro · insulin aspart · insulin glulisine)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|86009,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|325072,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|400008&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

**Query 2B — Basal / Long-Acting Insulin Analogs** *(insulin glargine · insulin detemir · insulin degludec)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|274783,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|274786,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1373463&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

**Query 2C — Human Insulin Formulations** *(regular human insulin · NPH / isophane insulin — older regimens more common in T1D on fixed-dose schedules)*
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5856,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5860&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### 🟡 GROUP 3 — Hemoglobin A1c ≥ 6.5% *(Broad — Diabetic Range; Covers T1D + T2D + Secondary Diabetes)*

**Query 3A — All Standard HbA1c LOINC Panels** *(4548-4: routine · 4549-2: electrophoresis · 17856-6: HPLC · 59261-8: IFCC protocol)*
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|4549-2,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|59261-8&value-quantity=ge6.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final,amended,corrected&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

## Reference Summary Table

| # | Query | Resource | Code System | Signal Specificity |
|---|-------|----------|-------------|-------------------|
| 1A | E10.x T1D (all subcodes) | Condition | ICD-10-CM `:below E10` | ✅ High — T1D definitive |
| 1B | Pre-existing T1D in pregnancy | Condition | ICD-10-CM `:below O24.0` | ✅ High — T1D definitive |
| 1C | T1D (all subtypes) | Condition | SNOMED `:below 46635009` | ✅ High — T1D definitive |
| 2A | Rapid-acting insulin analogs | MedicationRequest | RxNorm `86009, 325072, 400008` | ⚠️ Low — T1D + T2D |
| 2B | Basal insulin analogs | MedicationRequest | RxNorm `274783, 274786, 1373463` | ⚠️ Low — T1D + T2D |
| 2C | Human insulin (regular/NPH) | MedicationRequest | RxNorm `5856, 5860` | ⚠️ Low — T1D + T2D |
| 3A | HbA1c ≥ 6.5% | Observation | LOINC `4548-4, 4549-2, 17856-6, 59261-8` | ⚠️ Low — all diabetes types |

---

## Population Derivation Notes

**Recommended union logic:**
```
Final T1D Population =
    Group1_Patients (1A ∪ 1B ∪ 1C)          ← confirmed T1D
  ∪ (Group2_Patients ∩ Group1_Patients)      ← insulin Rx validated by T1D Dx
  ∪ (Group3_Patients ∩ Group1_Patients)      ← elevated A1c validated by T1D Dx
```

**Key caveats to communicate to your team:**

- **`:below` modifier** requires the FHIR server to have an active terminology service (e.g., SNOMED CT or ICD-10-CM hierarchy loaded). If not supported, you will need to enumerate individual leaf codes explicitly (e.g., E10.9, E10.621, E10.65, etc.).
- **Insulin queries return MedicationRequests**, not patient lists directly — extract `MedicationRequest.subject` references to get patient IDs. The `_include=MedicationRequest:patient` parameter causes Patient resources to be bundled in the same response for convenience.
- **MedicationRequest `code` parameter** searches `medicationCodeableConcept` (inline-coded medications). If your EHR stores medications as references to a `Medication` resource, append the chained alternative: `&medication.code=...` using the same RxNorm values.
- **HbA1c units**: If any labs report in mmol/mol (IFCC units), add a parallel query: `&value-quantity=ge48|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mmol/mol` (48 mmol/mol ≈ 6.5%).
- **All queries return paged bundles** — iterate `Bundle.link[rel="next"]` until exhausted for full population capture.
- **Groups 2 and 3 alone are insufficient** to distinguish T1D from insulin-treated T2D, as you noted — always intersect with Group 1 for the confirmed T1D cohort before CGM coverage determination.

## Two-Query Subtraction Approach

**Step A — Query 1: Collect all patients with insulin prescriptions (the base set)**

```
MedicationRequest?medication.ingredient-code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5856&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

**Step B — Query 2: Collect all patients with T1D *or* T2D diagnoses (the exclusion set)**

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009,http://snomed.info/sct|44054006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Subtraction Logic
> **Result = Patients(Query 1) − Patients(Query 2)**
> Extract `subject` references from each bundle, then return Patient IDs present in Step A but **absent** in Step B.

---

### Key Design Decisions

| Element | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `medication.ingredient-code` + RxNorm `5856` | Matches **any** insulin product (glargine, lispro, aspart, detemir, degludec, regular, etc.) by ingredient rather than individual product codes |
| `code:below` modifier | Traverses ICD-10-CM and SNOMED hierarchies — captures all subcategory codes (e.g., `E10.65`, `E11.9`, SNOMED children like "T1D with nephropathy") automatically |
| SNOMED `46635009` | Type 1 diabetes mellitus |
| SNOMED `44054006` | Type 2 diabetes mellitus |
| ICD-10-CM `E10` | Type 1 DM category (all E10.x subcodes via `:below`) |
| ICD-10-CM `E11` | Type 2 DM category (all E11.x subcodes via `:below`) |
| Comma-separated codes | OR logic within Query 2 — any T1D **or** T2D code triggers exclusion |
| `_include=...:subject` | Pulls Patient resources in same bundle for efficient ID extraction |

> **Note:** For efficiency at scale, replace `_include=...:subject` with `_elements=subject` to return only patient references without full Patient resource payloads.

### ⚠️ Critical Correction: `&code=` (AND) vs. `,` (OR)

Your proposed Query 2 uses `&code=…&code=…`, which FHIR interprets as **AND** (patient must match *both* parameter blocks simultaneously — impossible for mutually exclusive code systems on the same element). T1D ∪ T2D exclusion requires **OR logic**, achieved via comma-separation within a *single* `code` parameter, with each value fully qualified as `system|code`.

Additionally, SNOMED shorthand `code=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009,199229001` only applies the system prefix to the *first* code — subsequent bare values are treated as system-less. Every code needs its own `system|` prefix.

---

### Query 1 — Insulin Patients (MedicationRequest)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5856,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|274783,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|311036,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|253182,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|575068&_elements=subject
```

---

### Query 2a — T1D + T2D Diagnoses (SNOMED CT, OR logic)

All six codes comma-separated in **one** `code` parameter — fires on any match:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|46635009,http://snomed.info/sct|199229001,http://snomed.info/sct|23045005,http://snomed.info/sct|31321000119102,http://snomed.info/sct|190368000,http://snomed.info/sct|44054006&_elements=subject
```

### Query 2b — T1D + T2D Diagnoses (ICD-10-CM hierarchy, `:below` for E10.x + E11.x)

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&_elements=subject
```

> **Note:** Your original spec listed only `E11` (T2D). `E10` (T1D) is added here to mirror the SNOMED coverage — omitting it would create an asymmetry where ICD-10-coded T1D patients evade exclusion.

---

### Set Difference Logic

```
Result = patients(Q1) − ( patients(Q2a) ∪ patients(Q2b) )
```

| Step | Operation | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| Q1 | Insulin RxRequest → patient IDs | Source cohort |
| Q2a | SNOMED T1D + T2D → patient IDs | Exclusion set A |
| Q2b | ICD-10 E10.x + E11.x → patient IDs | Exclusion set B |
| Q2a ∪ Q2b | Union of exclusion sets | Full exclusion (both T1D **and** T2D codes required — neither alone is sufficient) |
| Q1 − (Q2a ∪ Q2b) | Set difference | **Insulin patients with no recorded T1D or T2D dx** |
